ribbondesc: Advancing the frontiers of computational multiphysics through innovative research and cutting-edge numerical methods.

Ongoing Sponsored Research.title: Our young research group is actively developing proposals in energetic materials, HPC solver development and shock-induced aerobreakup and combustion. We are building a strong foundation for future projects and welcome enthusiastic students, collaborators, and partners to engage with us as we grow.

Past Sponsored Research.title: Integrating Multiscale Modeling and Experiments to Develop a Meso-Informed Predictive Capability for Explosives Safety and Performance
Past Sponsored Research.source: U.S. Department of Defense, Air Force Office of Scientific Research (AFOSR), MURI Program
contributions: Higher Order Accurate Modeling of Micro/Meso/Macro scale simulations
New Framework to model Flyer Impact induced Shock Initiation of PBX
Physics Aware Recurrent Convolutional Neural Network
New sub-grid scale closure models (PARC & MISSEL) for Macro-Scale Sensitivity Predictions
Past Sponsored Research.notes: 4 papers published; 1 in Science Advances (IF=13.6), 5+ in progress

Past Sponsored Research.title: Development of a Multiscale Computational Framework for High Speed Multimaterial Dynamics

contributions: Uncertainty Quantification of Surrogate Burn Model MESIG
Published two-part paper in Journal of Applied Physics
Extending burn model to multi-dimensional prediction of corner turning and re-detonation

Past Sponsored Research.title: Physics-Informed Meta-Learning for Design of Complex Materials
contributions: Deep learning model to meta-learn thermo-mechanical response of CHNO explosives
Building material database of HMX, RDX, TNT, PETN, TATB
Running mesoscale simulations and training PhD students
